previously_flagged_now_resolved: 08_sympatric — Lake Victoria '>500 species' overclaim: resolved. Now reads 'African Great Lakes' with 'hundreds of species per lake,' which is defensible across Malawi, Tanganyika, and Victoria without picking the wrong record-holder.
07_allopatric — Galápagos finch count: resolved. Updated to 'around 17 species — more than a dozen' instead of the dated '13+' figure. Hedged phrasing absorbs ongoing taxonomic flux.

11_clades_compare — Archosauria cladistic inconsistency: resolved, and elegantly. The slide now explicitly says 'crocodilians + dinosaurs (which INCLUDES birds) + pterosaurs,' and the narration calls out that listing 'birds and dinosaurs' as separate groups is the wrong framing. This now teaches cladistic thinking instead of accidentally undermining it.

15_origin_of_life — Miller-Urey reducing atmosphere hedge: resolved. Both the slide content_hint and the narration now include the caveat that the assumed reducing mix is considered less accurate for early Earth, while preserving the core principle (abiotic monomer synthesis). Pitch is exactly right for AP — one clause, no rabbit hole.

14_extinction_variation — Northern elephant seal bottleneck number: resolved. Changed from '~20 individuals' to 'fewer than 100 individuals in the late 1800s,' which is the conservative, defensible figure.

08_sympatric — Allohexaploid wheat distinction: resolved, and stronger than I suggested. The outline now both labels bread wheat as 'allohexaploid (polyploid hybrid)' AND adds the explicit autopolyploidy vs allopolyploidy contrast in narration and vocabulary. This is a genuine pedagogical upgrade, not just a precision patch.

06_postzygotic_isolation — Mule sterility reasoning: resolved. The 'odd number can't pair' shorthand is gone; replaced with 'horse and donkey chromosomes are too different to pair correctly during meiosis,' which captures the underlying non-homology / rearrangement reality. Bonus: dropping the 64/62/63 chromosome counts (per Reviewer C) removes a numerical detail that was load-bearing for the now-deleted shorthand anyway.

15_origin_of_life — LUCA inference framing: resolved. Narration now reads 'we infer LUCA existed because every living thing today shares the same genetic code AND the same core molecular machinery — ribosomes, ATP, conserved proteins,' which is the stronger multi-line-of-evidence argument I wanted. The ~3.5 Bya date is retained as the conservative fossil-bound figure, which the revisor explicitly justified — I accept that call.
overall_assessment: All eight of my round-1 concerns are addressed substantively, not cosmetically. Several fixes are stronger than what I suggested — the Archosauria reframe with an explicit 'birds ARE dinosaurs' callout, the auto- vs allopolyploidy distinction promoted into both vocabulary and narration, and the LUCA inference now resting on shared core machinery rather than just the genetic code. The revisor also made defensible judgment calls on what to keep against other reviewers (Unit 7 weight, LUCA framing, hair-as-mammal-synapomorphy) with clear reasoning. The Galápagos-finches-as-pure-allopatric simplification I noted as 'fine for AP' was correctly left as-is per CED scope. The cichlid example loses a tiny bit of geographic specificity (Victoria vs African Great Lakes generally) but gains accuracy, which is the right trade for a teaching video. This outline is now production-ready from an evolutionary-biology precision standpoint. Verdict: pass.
